article_title: Cloudflare Retires 15 Years of NGINX, Ships Gen 13 Servers Built Around a 192-Core Turin and a Rust Proxy

content_preview.title: Cloudflare Retires 15 Years of NGINX, Ships Gen 13 Servers Built Around a 192-Core Turin and a Rust Proxy
content_preview.summary: Cloudflare's Gen 13 platform pairs AMD EPYC 9965 chips with FL2, a Rust rewrite of its NGINX-based proxy, doubling edge throughput while trading L3 cache for cores.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cloudflare has begun deploying its thirteenth-generation edge servers to more than 330 cities, pairing AMD's 192-core EPYC 9965 "Turin" processor with FL2, a complete Rust rewrite of the request-handling layer that has run on NGINX and LuaJIT for fifteen years. According to Cloudflare's [Gen 13 launch announcement](https://blog.cloudflare.com/gen13-launch/) on March 23, 2026, the new hardware doubles per-server throughput compared to Gen 12 while improving performance-per-watt by 50...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantial Analysis (1,062 words). Primary-sourced via three Cloudflare blog posts + InfoQ. All technical specs verified verbatim.

source_verification.https://blog.cloudflare.com/gen13-launch: source-0.html — Verified: March 23, 2026 launch, 192-core EPYC Turin (article specifies 'Zen 5c' which is bot's technical inference; source uses generic 'Zen 5'), 'Unacceptable' framing for FL1+9965, 2x core count vs Gen 12, FL2 swap erases latency penalty.
source_verification.https://blog.cloudflare.com/gen13-config: source-1.html — Verified verbatim: 768 GB DDR5-6400, 7.68 TB drives, 24 TB total storage, dual 100 GbE + OCP 3.0, 1300W Titanium PSU, Turin 9965, 'more than 330 cities'.
source_verification.https://blog.cloudflare.com/20-percent-i: source-2.html — Verified verbatim: NGINX/PHP origin, OpenResty/LuaJIT migration, FL2 in Rust on Oxy, 100+ engineers / 130+ modules, 10ms median latency reduction, 25% CDN benchmark gain, 'turn off FL1 in early 2026'.
source_verification.https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/04/cache: source-3.html — InfoQ corroborates the architectural shift framing (parallelism over cache).

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative specs verified. Minor: article specifies 'Zen 5c' core variant; sources use generic 'Zen 5'. The 9965 Turin Dense SKU technically uses Zen 5c, so the bot's clarification is correct, but it's an inference beyond the source text.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, technical. 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags lack of independent benchmarks and uncertain FL1-shutdown completion.
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. Companion to existing Pingora coverage but goes beyond it with hardware specifics.
recommendations: Optional: rephrase 'Zen 5c' to 'Zen 5 (Zen 5c dense variant in the 9965 SKU)' to flag the bot's technical clarification.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. High-quality cloud-infrastructure Analysis with primary-source verification end-to-end.
